description: Chocolate mousse is a light, airy dessert made by combining chocolate with whipped cream, beaten egg whites, or both. It is known for a smooth texture and rich chocolate flavor.
preparation_steps: Melt the chocolate and let it cool slightly.
Fold in whipped cream, beaten egg whites, or another aerating component.
Portion into serving dishes and chill until set.
Serve cold, often with cream or fruit.

health_advice.benefits: Rich flavor means a small serving can feel satisfying.
health_advice.risks: Often high in fat and sugar; some traditional recipes use raw or lightly cooked eggs.
health_advice.healthy_tip: Use small portions and refrigerate properly if eggs are used.

dietary_notes.note: Traditional versions are usually vegetarian and often naturally gluten-free. Vegan versions can be made with dairy-free chocolate and plant-based cream or aquafaba.
